capability_questions.capability_name: Service exists and is in good condition

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have an access management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have an access management service?

questions.prompt: Is the access management service reviewed frequently to measure its efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the access management service reviewed frequently to measure its efficacy?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise formalize and document all KPIs and KRIs for the access management service?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise formalize and document all KPIs and KRIs for the access management service?

questions.prompt: Are the KPIs and KRIs for access management reviewed frequently?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the KPIs and KRIs for access management reviewed frequently?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have technology or capabilities to manage access control for all enforcement points?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have technology or capabilities to manage access control for all enforcement points?
questions.evaluation_focus: Access control for all enforcement points

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a centralized decision point that makes access control decisions, or are decision makers siloed and specific to location?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a centralized decision point that makes access control decisions, or are decision makers siloed and specific to location?
questions.evaluation_focus: Centralized vs siloed policy decision points

questions.prompt: Can access control be dynamically changed and subsequently deployed to enforcement points?
questions.intent: Evaluate can access control be dynamically changed and subsequently deployed to enforcement points?

questions.prompt: Are access management system (PDP) configurations reviewed frequently to be considered in line with the respective policies (authentication, authorization, network access rules, application access rules, etc.)?
questions.intent: Evaluate are access management system (PDP) configurations reviewed frequently to be considered in line with the respective policies (authentication, authorization, network access rules, application access rules, etc.)?
questions.evaluation_focus: PDP configuration review aligned to policies

questions.prompt: Does the access management system have the capability to push abstracted policies to enforcement points that deploy the actual policy to their respective technology?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the access management system have the capability to push abstracted policies to enforcement points that deploy the actual policy to their respective technology?
questions.evaluation_focus: Abstracted policy push to enforcement points

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise consume existing policies to have a documented formalized process of provisioning need-to-know access for subjects?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise consume existing policies to have a documented formalized process of provisioning need-to-know access for subjects?

questions.prompt: Is the network access request process the same when requesting access across all locations?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the network access request process the same when requesting access across all locations?

questions.prompt: Is there an automated process with self-requesting portals to provision network access?
questions.intent: Evaluate is there an automated process with self-requesting portals to provision network access?

questions.prompt: Is the network access provisioning process reviewed frequently for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the network access provisioning process reviewed frequently for efficacy?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise consume existing endpoint and device policies to have a documented formalized process of provisioning need-to-know access for endpoint devices to specific enterprise assets?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise consume existing endpoint and device policies to have a documented formalized process of provisioning need-to-know access for endpoint devices to specific enterprise assets?

questions.prompt: Is the endpoint access request process the same when requesting access across all locations?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the endpoint access request process the same when requesting access across all locations?

questions.prompt: Is the endpoint access provisioning process reviewed frequently for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the endpoint access provisioning process reviewed frequently for efficacy?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise consume existing application policies to have a documented formalized process of provisioning need-to-know access to enterprise assets?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise consume existing application policies to have a documented formalized process of provisioning need-to-know access to enterprise assets?

questions.prompt: Is the process the same when requesting application access across all locations and application systems?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the process the same when requesting application access across all locations and application systems?
questions.evaluation_focus: Consistent application access across locations and systems

questions.prompt: Is the application access provisioning process reviewed frequently for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the application access provisioning process reviewed frequently for efficacy?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise adopt identity-based access control?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise adopt identity-based access control?

questions.prompt: Do all enforcement points support and deploy identity-based rules?
questions.intent: Evaluate do all enforcement points support and deploy identity-based rules?
questions.evaluation_focus: Identity-based rules at all enforcement points

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise consider device posture as part of network access based on the existing network access process?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise consider device posture as part of network access based on the existing network access process?

questions.prompt: Does subject posture get considered when accessing the network from all locations?
questions.intent: Evaluate does subject posture get considered when accessing the network from all locations?
questions.evaluation_focus: Subject posture considered from all locations

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise consider enterprise asset attributes as part of application access based on the existing application access process?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise consider enterprise asset attributes as part of application access based on the existing application access process?

questions.prompt: Are enterprise asset attributes considered when accessing applications for all subjects?
questions.intent: Evaluate are enterprise asset attributes considered when accessing applications for all subjects?
questions.evaluation_focus: Asset attributes considered for all subjects

capability_questions.capability_name: Remote access across untrusted networks based on identity and device posture

questions.prompt: Is there a documented and formalized access process for remote users in the enterprise?
questions.intent: Evaluate is there a documented and formalized access process for remote users in the enterprise?

questions.prompt: Do all remote users across all gateways (cloud, data center, on-premises) have the same access policy? Is the access policy the same if the same user accesses the network from a trusted LAN segment?
questions.intent: Evaluate do all remote users across all gateways (cloud, data center, on-premises) have the same access policy? Is the access policy the same if the same user accesses the network from a trusted LAN segment?
questions.evaluation_focus: Consistent remote access policy across gateways and LAN

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise allow for advanced technology like cloud SSE to allow access to internal trusted enterprise assets?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise allow for advanced technology like cloud SSE to allow access to internal trusted enterprise assets?
questions.evaluation_focus: Cloud SSE for internal asset access

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise allow access for enterprise assets from anywhere over the internet without VPN?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise allow access for enterprise assets from anywhere over the internet without VPN?

capability_questions.capability_name: Access Isolation (Separation of duties) and its automation

questions.prompt: Is there a clear process of user separation between production, development, and deployment?
questions.intent: Evaluate is there a clear process of user separation between production, development, and deployment?
questions.evaluation_focus: User separation across production, development, and deployment

questions.prompt: Are service accounts and normal accounts given separate permissions?
questions.intent: Evaluate are service accounts and normal accounts given separate permissions?
questions.evaluation_focus: Separate permissions for service and normal accounts

questions.prompt: Does the CI/CD pipeline remove development users from access to production automatically when code gets pushed to production?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the CI/CD pipeline remove development users from access to production automatically when code gets pushed to production?
questions.evaluation_focus: Automated CI/CD removal of dev access from production

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a granular access control matrix for subject to object?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a granular access control matrix for subject to object?

questions.prompt: Does the access matrix apply to all locations and functions and not just specific segments?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the access matrix apply to all locations and functions and not just specific segments?

questions.prompt: Are the access control matrices reviewed frequently for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate are the access control matrices reviewed frequently for efficacy?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a formalized, documented external or guest user onboarding process?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a formalized, documented external or guest user onboarding process?

questions.prompt: Does guest user access timeframe and access control provisioning and deprovisioning happen automatically?
questions.intent: Evaluate does guest user access timeframe and access control provisioning and deprovisioning happen automatically?

questions.prompt: Is there a documented and formalized process to provide subject access to the OOB network?
questions.intent: Evaluate is there a documented and formalized process to provide subject access to the OOB network?

questions.prompt: Are secure protocols used to access OOB?
questions.intent: Evaluate are secure protocols used to access OOB?

questions.prompt: Does the subject get the same access control to OOB irrespective of the source location (LAN, VPN, cloud)?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the subject get the same access control to OOB irrespective of the source location (LAN, VPN, cloud)?
questions.evaluation_focus: Consistent OOB access control across source locations

questions.prompt: Are OOB access controls reviewed frequently for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate are OOB access controls reviewed frequently for efficacy?

questions.prompt: Is there a documented and formalized process to provide subject access to data?
questions.intent: Evaluate is there a documented and formalized process to provide subject access to data?

questions.prompt: Is access provided to data the same for the subject irrespective of location?
questions.intent: Evaluate is access provided to data the same for the subject irrespective of location?

questions.prompt: Is the data access process and policy reviewed frequently for efficacy?
questions.intent: Evaluate is the data access process and policy reviewed frequently for efficacy?

questions.prompt: Does the enterprise have a separate service manager for access management, distinct from authentication, credential management, and Active Directory management, to maintain separation of duties?
questions.intent: Evaluate does the enterprise have a separate service manager for access management, distinct from authentication, credential management, and Active Directory management, to maintain separation of duties?

capability_questions.capability_name: Access, authorization and accounting and logging

questions.prompt: Is there a formal documented process for logging access logs, activity, and lifecycle changes to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate is there a formal documented process for logging access logs, activity, and lifecycle changes to the SOC?

questions.prompt: Is logging of access logs and activity made securely to the SOC? (This is different from authentication logs or credential creation logs.)
questions.intent: Evaluate is logging of access logs and activity made securely to the SOC? (This is different from authentication logs or credential creation logs.)
questions.evaluation_focus: Secure access activity logging to SOC

questions.prompt: Are dynamic changes to access logs and activity captured and transferred to the SOC?
questions.intent: Evaluate are dynamic changes to access logs and activity captured and transferred to the SOC?
